reasoning: As of November 9, 2025, the Iranian regime remains in control of the central government. The regime recently survived a significant external shock (a "12-Day War" with Israel in June 2025) and a ceasefire is in place. Furthermore, recent reports (Aug-Oct 2025) note that while there is widespread discontent, large-scale protests have not broken out and there is no clear, organized opposition movement capable of capturing power. Succession planning for Khamenei is underway, but a leadership change within the existing system explicitly resolves as NO. With only 52 days remaining in 2025, the base rate for an established autocracy collapsing in such a short window is practically zero. Factoring in current stability following the June war, the probability of complete regime change before year-end is extremely low, hitting the 2% floor.
